front: Poem: opens 'All human things are subject to decay'
Poem: satirizes Thomas Shadwell who 'never deviates into sense'
Poem: Duke of Monmouth and Earl of Shaftesbury satirized as biblical figures rebelling against David
Poem: allegorical — Hind = Catholic Church, Panther = Anglican
back: The Hind and the Panther (John Dryden)

front: Poem: milk-white creature 'immortal and unchang'd'
back: The Hind and the Panther (John Dryden)

front: Play: adaptation of Antony and Cleopatra set entirely in Alexandria
Play: 'men are but children of a larger growth'
Author: first Poet Laureate under Charles II
Author: received 'butt of Canary wine'
Author: wrote Annus Mirabilis about the Great Fire of London
Poem: Shadwell advised to retire to "some peaceful province in acrostic land"
Poem: Pope's Dunciad inspired by this mock-epic
Poem: opens "In pious times, ere priestcraft did begin"
Poem: Buckingham depicted as servant Zimri; Nahum Tate co-wrote Part 2
Poem: fable of Swallows convinced not to migrate — they all die in cold
back: The Hind and the Panther (John Dryden)

front: Play: Dolabella muses "men are but children of a larger growth"; eunuch Alexas delivers ruby bracelet
Play: Cleopatra kills herself with dagger not snake — departing from Shakespeare
Author: Alexander's Feast — "None but the brave deserve the fair"; set to music by Handel
Author: Astraea Redux — praised Charles II as "happy prince" after Restoration
Author: wrote libretto for Purcell's King Arthur semi-opera with "Frost Scene"
Author: collaborated with Davenant on The Enchanted Island (adaptation of The Tempest)
